Web Design Services

Websites that look goodand actually convert.

Built from scratch around how your buyers actually decide. Not a theme with your brand colors swapped in.

Start Your ProjectView Our Work

Most business websites look fine and convert at 1 to 2%

A pretty site that does not generate leads is an expense. Templates and DIY builders get you online fast, but they skip the strategy that turns traffic into revenue.

  • Homepages try to say everything and guide visitors nowhere specific.
  • Contact forms sit at the bottom of long pages with no reason to fill them out above the fold.
  • Mobile experience breaks on key pages even though most traffic arrives on a phone.
  • Slow load times hurt ad quality and SEO while visitors bounce before the page finishes.

Websites designed around your sales process

  • Page architecture based on your services, buyer questions, and conversion goals.
  • Custom design that reflects your brand without sacrificing speed or usability.
  • Conversion elements placed where visitors actually decide: above the fold, mid-page, and at exit.
  • Core Web Vitals optimization so your site performs in search and paid campaigns.

What Makes a Kinexus Build Different

Conversion paths sketched first, performance benchmarks enforced, and a handoff your marketing team can run without us.

Desktop
PageSpeed
94/100
Responsive
✓
01

Conversion before aesthetics

We sketch the CTA path first. Pretty comes second. Awards do not pay your payroll.

02

Performance is non-negotiable

Lighthouse 90 or higher. Core Web Vitals green. Your site loads before people lose patience.

03

Mobile-first, not mobile-adapted

Wireframes start on a phone screen because that is where most of your traffic lives.

04

Handoff your team can run

CMS training, component docs, and a site your marketing team can update without calling us every week.

The build process.

From discovery to launch, every phase has a clear deliverable and a defined success metric.

  1. Phase 1

    Discovery & Strategy

    User behavior analysis, competitor audit, stakeholder interviews. Output: strategic brief and sitemap.

    Strategic brief delivered in week 1

  2. Phase 2

    UX & Wireframing

    High-fidelity wireframes mapping every user journey. Navigation flows tested before a single pixel is designed.

    Every user journey mapped before design

  3. Phase 3

    Visual Design

    Full-color mockups rooted in conversion psychology. Each block has a clear hierarchy and purpose.

    Conversion-focused mockups in 2 weeks

  4. Phase 4

    Development & QA

    Clean code, Lighthouse 90+, WCAG accessible. Cross-browser tested on every device.

    Lighthouse 90+ on every build

  5. Phase 5

    Launch & Handoff

    Zero-downtime deployment, DNS migration, CMS training. Your team can hit the ground running.

    Zero-downtime deployment guaranteed

Web design project deliverables

Discovery through go-live. Every deliverable serves the same goal: qualified visitors who take action.

Scope of work

  • Discovery and UX Strategy

    Buyer research, sitemap, wireframes, and conversion path mapping before any design work starts.

  • Custom Visual Design

    Brand-aligned mockups for key pages with mobile and desktop layouts, no templates.

  • Responsive Development

    Clean, fast code on modern frameworks with CMS integration for easy content updates.

  • Conversion-Focused Page Templates

    Service pages, landing pages, and contact flows built with forms, CTAs, and trust signals.

  • On-Page SEO Foundation

    Title tags, meta descriptions, schema markup, and site structure ready for organic growth.

  • Launch Support and Training

    QA testing, analytics setup, CMS training, and 30 days of post-launch adjustments.

Proof it works

Trust signals from real client work, not vanity metrics.

Case Study

Landscaping Company

Template site with 45% bounce rate and no clear CTA above the fold.

4.8× lead growth after custom rebuild. Conversion rate went from 1.8% to 8.4% with Lighthouse 94 score.

Transparent Pricing

See what's included at each tier.

Fixed scope. No surprise fees on the invoice.

Common Questions

FAQ

How long does a website project take?>

Most projects run 6–10 weeks: 1 week discovery, 1–2 weeks UX and wireframing, 2–3 weeks visual design, 2–3 weeks development and QA, then launch. Complex sites with e-commerce or custom functionality may take longer.

Do you build on specific platforms or CMS?>

We build custom sites and work with WordPress and Webflow depending on your team's needs and the complexity of the project. We recommend the right stack for your goals, not the one that's easiest for us.

Will my site be fast and mobile-optimized?>

Yes. Every site we build hits Lighthouse 90+ on mobile and desktop, passes Core Web Vitals, and meets WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards. Performance is baked into the build process, not bolted on at the end.

Do you handle content migration from my existing site?>

Yes. If you have existing content worth keeping, we migrate and restructure it as part of the build. We'll audit your current content and recommend what to keep, update, or retire.

Can you redesign an existing website?>

Yes. Redesigns are a common engagement. We audit your current site, identify what's working and what isn't, and rebuild it with a clear conversion strategy and modern performance standards.

How much does a custom website cost?>

Most business websites range from $8,000 to $35,000 depending on page count, custom functionality, and integrations. E-commerce and multi-location sites may exceed that range. We provide a fixed quote after discovery.

How long does a web design project take?>

Standard business websites take 6 to 10 weeks from kickoff to launch. Larger sites with custom features may take 10 to 14 weeks. We set a clear timeline in your proposal with milestone check-ins.

Do you redesign existing websites or only build new ones?>

Both. We redesign underperforming sites and build new ones from scratch. Redesigns often include a conversion audit first so we fix what is broken before changing the design.

What CMS do you build on?>

We primarily build on Next.js and headless CMS platforms for speed and flexibility. For clients who need WordPress, we build custom themes without page builder bloat.
